content.method: Difference-in-differences analysis using natural variation in past ministerial denials of track-expansion requests as a mini-pilot; track substitution flows into church/foundation-sector enrollment following past capacity changes; if data permit, a small-scale regional pilot with capacity caps in matched treatment/control counties, monitoring cross-sector enrollment shifts.
content.question: If selective-track capacity or entry points are contracted or delayed, how much of the current segregation would be displaced into church/foundation-school admissions, private tutoring markets, within-school classroom sorting, or residential sorting rather than genuinely reduced?
content.why_it_matters: This determines whether T2 (contraction) and T3 (structural delay) achieve real equity gains or merely relocate sorting to less-regulated, less-visible channels outside Klebelsberg jurisdiction—directly affecting expected net benefit and monitoring design for both proposals.
